sutta: 157 the buddha said monks there are these 2 diseases what 2 disease of body and disease of mind monks there are to be seen beings who can admit freedom from suffering from bodily disease for 1 year for 2 years for 3 4 5 10 20 30 40 50 years who can admit freedom from bodily disease for even 100 years but monks those beings are hard to find in the world who can admit freedom from mental disease even for 1 moment save only those in whom the asavas are destroyed
commentary:
i'll just stop here to commend here the buddha is guys you can have bodily health you know you can be very healthy in the body but mentally yeah most of us are we are sick in the mind all the time practically all the time and this is saying that only the arahan 1 who has destroyed the asavasana only the arahant is saying we are all in a way insane you know or something like that as they say 1 screw loose so it's uh very important for us to cultivate our mind after all uh the body however much you take care of the body is finally you'll be ungrateful to you he'll break down and it'll give you a lot of pain but the mind if we cultivate our mind can bring us a lot of happiness the buddha said that an uncultivated mind is um is a source of great suffering a great sorrow whereas a cultivated mind brings you a lot of bliss a lot of joy a lot of happiness and not only that you can bring that with us now into our next uh birth and our next rebirth in our next life then the buddha continued monks there are these 4 diseases of 1 who has gone forth from the worldly life what for here monks we may have a greedy 1 full of vexation and discontented with this or that supply of rope and arms food lodging seed medicines and requisites for sickness he being greedy full of axation discontented conceives an evil longing for consideration for gain honest and fame he rouses himself exerts himself makes an effort to get these things of sad purpose he visits families of sad purpose he takes a seat of sad purpose he speaks dharma of sad purpose he restrains the calls of nature these monks are the 4 diseases of him who has gone forth therefore monks thus must you train yourselves we will not become greedy full of vexation discontented with this or that supply of rope and arms food lodging seed medicines and requisites for sickness we will not conceive an evil longing for consideration for gain for honest and fame we will not rouse ourselves exert ourselves make an effort to get these things we will become enduring of cold hunger thirst contact of lies and mosquitoes of wind and rain and creeping things we will become bearers of abusive pain-causing ways of speech we will submit to painful bodily feelings grievous sharp wrecking distracting and discomforting that drain the life away thus monks must you train yourselves so here in the last part the buddha is saying that monks should not be full of greed and discontented and if a man is full of greed and once a name and fame wants a lot of uh offerings etc then um it's a disease and so the buddha told his disciples and generally to go to seclusion and practice at least for the the buddha encourages monks to practice for 10 years before they are allowed to take disciples in the vinaya rules a monk is not allowed to take any disciple until he has completed 10 years as a monk after the higher ordination then he has 10 years of experience and practice behind him then only he can take disciples there's a great danger in the veneration and offerings that among receives and it's quite easy for a monk to go astray the only protection is meditation samadhi to keep the mind strong otherwise it is quite easy to go astray for a month

because of some woman [Music] today we come to 4.16 and this i won't read on the whole sutra just a portion of it the buddha said monks these 4 things conduce to the confusion to the vanishing away of sat dharma through dharma what for in this case monks the monks get by heart a text that is wrongly taken with words and sense that are wrongly arranged now monks if words and sense are wrongly arranged the meaning also is misleading this is the first thing which conduces to the confusion through the vanishing away of saddam true dhamma then again the monks are difficult to speak to possessed of qualities which make them difficult to speak to they are stubborn incapable of being instructed this monks is the second thing then again those monks who are widely learned burst in the doctrines who know dhamma by heart who know vinaya by heart who know the summaries by heart these do not dutifully hand on attacks to another thus when they pass away the text is cut down at the root it has nothing to stand on this is the third thing yet again the elder monks live in abundance they are legs taking the lead in backsliding shirking the burden of the secluded life they set going no effort to attain the unattained to win the goal not 1 to realize the unrealized so the generation that follows comes to follow their view that generation also lives in abundance his legs takes the lead in backsliding to the worldly life shirking the burden of the secluded life descend going no effort to attain the unattained to win the goal not 1 to realize the unrealized this monks is the fourth thing which conduces to the confusion to the vanishing away of saddam true dhamma and that's the end of what i want to read in this suta so here this is 1 of the few sutas now where the buddha gives the reasons why the true dharma would vanish away in the future the first 1 is the words and sense of the dhamma are wrongly arranged and the meeting meaning also is misleading um this 1 could refer to the [Music] wrong teachings that the buddha predicted would appear 500 years after the buddha's parinibana and because of what the buddha calls counterfeit teachings false teachings that appear and multiplied um so this is 1 reason for the vanishing of the way of the true dhamma so we have to study the dhamma and know which are the wrong teachings it is not easy because we have to study quite a lot to know which teachings are contradictory which teachings are consistent and if we do that now we'll find that the earliest 4 nikayas those are the books which are very consistent whereas later books there are contradictions in the later books and the second reason is that the monks are not interested in the training the buddha always talked about the training for among zika pada the practice of the holy life and there are some monks who are stubborn and they refuse to change their bad habits they won't take instructions and so because of that they cause people to have to lose faith in the sangha and when people lose faith in the sangha they turn away from the religion so that is why like in the forest tradition in thailand they don't ordain somebody very easily somebody who wants to ordain as a monk first they come in as what they call a pacow 1 who wears the white cloth and that is 8 precepts they keep the 8 precepts for a minimum of 6 months and then after that only uh they are given the salmonera precepts and then after after at least 6 months as a summoner then they ordained in the higher ordination so this gives time mature to to examine the candidate to see whether that person is really suitable for the high ordination then the third reason is that the senior monks who are learned in the dhamma vinaya they don't hand the text to another in the earlier days and the dhamma the sutas was not put down in writing so it was transmitted from the verbally from 1 monk to another so if the earlier monks they became if they were lazy and they did not take the trouble to memorize the sutasa and hand down from 1 generation to another the sutas would have disappeared so now the sutas are in writings are in writing but now we have to take take the trouble to teach the dhamma vinaya the sutas and the vinaya so that they are well propagated so that people know the true teachings of the buddha so people are not confused with wrong teachings so it is our duty to teach and also to spread by perhaps printing of books by tapes etc then the fourth reason for the disappearance of the true dharma is the senior monks tehrasana they live in abundance they are legs they take the lead in backsliding and they don't make any effort to attain the goal of the holy life which is to end our suffering to end the round of rebirths so because the senior monks set that example the junior monks also follow them and become lags and live in luxury etc so this is another reason why the true dharma would pass away so we have to be careful of these reasons and try to [Music] lead our life and conduct ourselves so that we can preserve the true dharma now we come to suta number
